response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile individual contributor with a genuine developer-tools and testing bent — he authored a test runner and assertion library, built CI/dev-ops infrastructure, taught test-automation courses, and currently works on AI developer tooling. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows no people-management experience at all and no evidence of leading teams, performance management, or organizational change. His dev-tools work is also largely open-source side projects rather than sustained ownership of build/test infrastructure for a large platform ecosystem.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Authentic testing and developer-tooling background (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, CI/GitLab infrastructure, Selenium training, MCP code generators)
Mobile platform exposure (iOS and Apple TV apps) plus a record of teaching and knowledge-sharing
concerns: No people-management experience, the core requirement of this manager role
No demonstrated formal technical-leadership role, roadmap ownership, or organizational-change experience
Developer-tools work is mostly personal/open-source projects rather than professional build-systems or engineering-productivity work at scale

key_factors.factor: No formal technical-leadership or org-change track record
